jstats 0.9.82
  • Reference

Internal helper: parse a label-spec string into a named numeric vector

dot-jst_parse_labels.Rd

Parses a labels string of the form "1=Young; 2=Middle Aged; 3=Older" into a named numeric vector formatted for use with haven_labelled variables (names = label text, values = numeric codes). Splits on the first equals sign in each rule, so label text may itself contain equals signs.

Usage

.jst_parse_labels(labels_str)

Arguments

labels_str

Character string of the form "value1=label1; value2=label2; ...".

Value

Invisibly, a named numeric vector. Names are label strings; values are numeric codes, or Stata-style missing values for tagged entries.

Details

The left-hand side of each rule may be a numeric value or a Stata- style missing-value token (.a through .z). Tagged-NA entries are stored as haven::tagged_na(<letter>) values in the returned vector; callers can detect them via haven::na_tag().

On this page
